qt: Which of the following sets is uncountable?
What is recursion in mathematics?
The Cartesian product of two denumerable sets is:
The set of all possible ordered pairs of natural numbers is:
Which set is NOT an example of a countable set?
In a recursive sequence, each term is defined as a function of ___________.
op: Both the preceding term and the first term

qt: Countable sets include:
Which of the following statements about countable sets is FALSE?
op: Every subset of a countable set is countable.
Countable sets have a cardinality equal to that of the natural numbers.

qt: Which of the following sets is countable?
Two sets A = {pencil, copy} and B = {history, maths}are ________ sets.
If one set in a Cartesian product is uncountable, then the Cartesian product is:
Which of the following statement holds for a set of rational numbers?
op: It does not contain any denumerable subset.

qt: If a lower bound of A precdes every other lower bound of A, then it is called the infimum of A
Let A be an ordered set. Then A is said to be well-ordered if every subset of A contains a first element.
The ordinal number of the set P of counting numbers is denoted by ω.
Any well-ordered set A is not linearly ordered.
Let A be a well-ordered set. The initial segment s(a) of an element a ϵ A consists of all elements in A which strictly precedes a.
Let S be a partially ordered set, and let A be a subset of S. If A has an upper bound ,we say A is bounded above.
The ordinal number of set {a,{b,c}} is __________________.
Let A be a well-ordered set. Then If B is similar to A, then B is well-ordered.

Let S(A) denote the collection of all initial segments of elements in a well-ordered set A, and let S(A) be ordered by set inclusion. Then A is similar to S(A) and, in particular, the function f : A →S(A) defined by f(x) = s(x) is a similarity mapping between A and S(A).
Consider the usual ordering ≤ of the positive integers P = {1, 2, 3,…} and the negative integers A = {-1, -2, -3, …}. Then P is not order-isomorphic to A.

Consider a collection C of well-ordered sets. Each well-ordered set A in C is assigned a symbol in such a way that any two well-ordered sets A and B are assigned the same symbol if and only if A and B are similar. This symbol is called the ordinal number of A.
The ordinal number of set {1,2,3} is _____________.
Suppose A and B are well-ordered sets, and suppose A is similar to an initial segment of B. Then A is said to be _________ than B.
The set of positive integers P = {1, 2, 3, …} is not order-isomorphic to the set of even positive integers E = {2, 4, 6, …}.

Two ordered sets X and Y are said to be order-isomorphic or isomorphic or similar if there exists a one-to-one correspondence f : X → Y which preserves the order relations. Such a function f is then called an order-isomorphism or isomorphism from X onto Y.
An element 'a' in a well-ordered set A is called a limit element if ______________________.
op: it has an immediate predecessor and if it is the first element.
it does not has an immediate predecessor and if it is not the first element.

qt: Let S be a partially ordered set, and let A be a subset of S. If A has a lower bound we say A is bounded below.
Let C be a non-empty set of non-empty sets. A function f : C → ∪C is called a choice function for C if f(A) ϵ A for all A ϵ C.
The ordinal number of empty set is __________.

Let C be the set of countries on earth, thinking of each country as a collection of cities. Then ∪C is the set of all cities on earth, and the function f that assigns to each country its capital city is a choice function for C.
Suppose A and B are well-ordered sets, and suppose A is similar to an initial segment of B. Then B is said to be ________than A.
Let C be the collection of all pairs of shoes in the world. Then the function that picks the left shoe out of each pair is a choice function for C.

op: A has only an upper bound.
A has only a lower bound.
A has an upper and lower bound.

qt: If an upper bound of A succeeds every other upper bound of A, then it is called the supremum of A
The ordinal number of {1,2,3,4} is _________.
Let λ and μ be ordinal numbers and let A and B be two well-ordered sets such that λ = ord(A) and μ = ord(B). Then λ < μ if A is similar to an initial segment of B.
Let A be a well-ordered set. Then every subset of A is well-ordered.

Suppose X and Y are partially ordered sets. A one-to-one(injective) function f :X → Y is called a similar mapping from X into Y if f preserves the order relation, that is, if the following conditions holds for any pair a, b ϵ X: a ≤ b in X if and only if f(a) ≤ f(b) in Y.
Let S be a partially ordered set, and let A be a subset of S. An element n in S is called a lower bound of A if n precedes every element of A, that is, for every y ϵ A, we have y ≥ n.
Let S be a partially ordered set, and let A be a subset of S. An element m in S is called an upper bound of A if m succeeds every element of A, that is, for every x ϵ A, we have x ≤ m.
What is the defining property of an equivalence relation R on a set S?

What does [a] ≠ [b] imply in the context of an equivalence relation R on set S?
op: a and b are not elements of set S.
a and b are distinct equivalence classes under R.

qt: What does [a] = [b] signify in the context of an equivalence relation R on set S?
op: a and c are equivalent under R
a and b are equivalent under R

It indicates an ordered pair in the Cartesian plane.
It signifies a subset of set S.

qt: Let A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} and f:A→A is defined by f(x)=x, what is the range of the function?
Let A = {a, b, c}, B = {1, 2, 3, 4} and f: A → B such that f= {(a, 1), (b, 2), (c, 3)} be a function. What is f(b)?
In an equivalence relation, if (a, b) is in R, what can be said about (b, a)?
op: It is not related to (a, b).
It is in R due to symmetry.
